method: foldseek easy-search TM-align (--alignment-type 1) on marvin, NKG7 human Q16617 + mouse Q99PA5 AF monomers vs PDB100 and AFDB/Swiss-Prot; top hits' matched-chain molecule names resolved via RCSB data API.
key_findings: NKG7 fold's entire top structural neighborhood (TM-score ~0.87-0.90) is the claudin/PMP-22 + Ca_V/TARP-gamma tetraspan superfamily.

Top-20 PDB100 hits (human): 10 claudins (claudin-3/4/9/15/19) + 10 Ca_V/TARP gamma auxiliary subunits (CACNG1/2/3/5, stargazin/TARP; incl. GluA2-gamma chimeras whose MATCHED chain is the gamma subunit).

CRITICAL rigor: every 'calcium channel' hit matches the gamma AUXILIARY subunit, NOT a pore. 3jbr matched chain E = CACNG1 (Ca_V1.1 gamma), NOT the alpha1S pore (chain A). 9b5z matched chain F = CACNG2, NOT GluA2.
ZERO hits to a bona-fide standalone transmembrane ion-CONDUCTING channel pore (all 20 human top hits are either paracellular claudins or non-conducting Ca_V/TARP-gamma auxiliary subunits).
Sequence identity to all hits is low (fident ~0.10-0.19) — similarity is fold-level, consistent with distant superfamily membership, not close homology.
Mouse replicate reproduces the human result.
channel_hypothesis_bearing: mixed (confirms channel-ADJACENT fold family; but the specific structural analogs are all non-conducting auxiliary subunits / paracellular tetraspans, not pore-formers)
gate_recommendation: escalate — fold confirms the claudin/Ca_V-gamma tetraspan architecture (Tier-1 oligomer search warranted), but the negative (no pore-forming-channel structural analog) foreshadows a likely scaffold/auxiliary role.
